Small Batch Roaster
Curious Coffee · Ann Arbor, MI

Coffee that makes
you ask questions.

Single-origin. Farm-direct. Roasted in obsessively small batches.

Explore the Roast Shop Now
The Story

High quality coffee. Coffee education.

01

One cherry, two beans.

Curious Coffee has two main pillars: high quality coffee and coffee education. Two pursuits, one passion. Everything we do connects back to these roots.

02

The coffee is the heart of it.

We source exceptional green coffees and roast them light to make them shine. No dark roasting to hide flaws. Just great coffee, treated with care and respect.

03

For curious coffee minds.

We run coffee courses to suit every level. Whether you are just starting out or dialling in your espresso technique, we want to share everything we know.

Roasting Approach

Light roasts.
Big flavours.

The coffee is the heart of it. I aim to source exceptional green coffees and roast them light to make them shine.

Light roasting preserves the unique character that each farmer has worked so hard to develop. It keeps the brightness, the complexity, the story of where the coffee came from.

Every roast is small enough that we can control every variable. No shortcuts. If it is not exceptional, it does not make the cut.

Meet the Owner: Mike Bawden

Why We Are Different

This is what curiosity tastes like.

Obsessive Detail

We cup every roast. If it is not exceptional, it does not make the cut.

Farm Direct

We source from specific farms, specific people. Every bag tells a real story.

Flavor First

We chase unusual, delicious, and surprising. Not safe. Curious.

Currently in the Roastery

What is in the bag right now.

Our lineup rotates as we find exceptional lots. Hover any card to learn more about the coffee and the farm.

Huila, Colombia

Bella Alejandria geisha

Geisha

Premium
FloralSweet orangeChamomile
$38.50 Buy This Bag
Huila, Colombia

Bella Alejandria geisha

Intense florality, incredible sweetness and a long clean finish! When we tasted this coffee we knew it was just perfect for our special anniversary release! Farm: Bella Alejandria Origin: Huila, Colombia Variety: Geisha Process: anaerobic washed Notes: floral, sweet orange, chamomile Brewing: roasted primarily for filter coffee, but can also definitely be brewed as espresso. Rest from roast date: 3 weeks or more ideally. 14 days minimum. This coffee is from the producer Wilder Lazo and named after his farm "Bella Alejandria". It has pronounced floral notes including rose, jasmine, chamomile, hibiscus and more. It also has a restrained acidity which further accentuates the perception of sweetness in the cup. The anaerobic fermentation of this coffee emphasizes the character of the coffee. Wilder Lasso ensures that the soil is very well supplied with nutrients. Then, the fully ripe, extremely large fruits are harvested and washed in water tanks to separate them from impurities and floating beans. The cherries were pulped and anaerobically fermented in mucilage for just 36 hours. After washing, the coffee dried on raised beds for 12 days. With this minimal fermentation time, and Wilder's controlled techniques, the result is incredibly clean rather than a process-forward result. Wilder's intention was that this Geisha should taste just like the plain variety and not overly processed: floral and delicate. This is why he chose an anaerobic dry fermentation. Submerged fermentation results in more deep florals, richer body, but dry fermentation highlights brightness and bright florals. Mind blowing as filter and also as espresso. To avoid wasting too much dialing in your espresso, I would consider pulling turbo shots with this as they are very forgiving. 6oz/170g bags

Buy Now $38.50
Huila, Colombia

Dinastía geisha

Geisha

Premium
StrawberryWhite chocolateTropical fruits!
$38.50 Buy This Bag
Huila, Colombia

Dinastía geisha

What a stunner! This natural process geisha is spectacular! Farm: La Dinastía Origin: Huila, Colombia Variety: Geisha Process: anaerobic natural Notes: strawberry, white chocolate, tropical fruits! Brewing: roasted primarily for filter coffee, but can also definitely be brewed as espresso. Rest from roast date: 2 weeks or more ideally. 10 days minimum. This coffee is from the producer Wilder Lazo and named after his farm "La Dinastía". It is the second of our one year anniversary releases and compliments the florality of the Bella Alejandria geisha with bold tropical fruit notes. When you grind this coffee the aroma of strawberries and tropical fruit immediately greets you. When hot the strawberry and white chocolate notes prevail, but as it cools it shifts to passion fruit and other tropical fruits and more pronounced florals. (That sounds so pretentious but it's really true!) This isn't a super funky natural process coffee and doesn't have any pronounced boozy notes so it should please those who love naturals and those who often stick to washed coffees alike. 6oz/170g bags

Honduras, La Piedrona, Chinacla, La Paz

El Puente SL-28

SL-28

Premium
Yellow plumJasminePomeloHerbaceous
$25.00 Buy This Bag
Honduras, La Piedrona, Chinacla, La Paz · 1580 masl

El Puente SL-28

A captivating washed coffee with a real depth of flavour to decipher and explore for fans of low intervention washed coffees. Origin: Honduras, La Piedrona, Chinacla, La Paz Farm: El Puente Altitude: 1580 masl Producers: Moises Herrera and Marysabel Caballero Variety: SL-28 Process: low intervention washed Notes: yellow plum, jasmine, pomelo, herbaceous, rhubarb, ginger and more Brewing: Roasted light with filter in mind but for lovers of light roasts this also makes for a delicious espresso. Rest: this coffee likes a solid three weeks rest from the roast date to really open up. This coffee has a bright acidity and a medium sweetness. Under close observation you can pin down an array of tasting notes. I love the subtlety of this one and it keeps me coming back for more. 6oz/170g bags

Minas Gerais, Brazil

Fazenda Guariroba pink bourbon

pink bourbon

Standard
FloralsSweet gingerPepperChocolate and more!
$27.50 Buy This Bag
Minas Gerais, Brazil · 1200 masl

Fazenda Guariroba pink bourbon

A very different Brazilian coffee! Medium acidity, high sweetness, clean cup. Farm: GuarirobaProcess: Double Fermentation* Variety: pink bourbon Producers: Gabriel Lamounier Vieira and Elisa Paiva Lamounier Region: Minas Gerais, Brazil Elevation: 1200 masl Notes: florals, sweet ginger, pepper, chocolate and more! Brewing methods: This coffee is roasted light with filter brews in mind. It can definitely also work well for espresso, but if you’re looking for that more ‘classic’ Brazilian profile, then I would choose the Fazenda Sao Domingos – also available on my site. When you grind this coffee you are immediately greeted by a waft of delicate spices. Up front you have florals and spice. The finish has some more familiar chocolate notes that one might expect from a Brazilian coffee. When profiling the roast, I found that slightly darker roasts elevated the mouthfeel and chocolate while very light roasts boosted clarity and florals. My iteration leans a little to the latter while preserving the sweet chocolate finish. *The coffee undergoes an initial hydraulic separation, followed by fermentation in a bioreactor for 36 hours with the addition of carefully selected and isolated microorganisms sourced from the same lot. After pulping, the coffee undergoes a second fermentation for an additional 36 hours. Drying is conducted over approximately 20 days to ensure optimal flavor development. 10oz/285g bags

Guatemala, Palencia

Finca El Socorro - low caffeine

Laurina

Standard
Toasted almondsNougatRed apple
$28.50 Buy This Bag
Guatemala, Palencia

Finca El Socorro - low caffeine

A sweet, smooth and delicate coffee from the El Socorro farm in Guatemala. Origin: Guatemala, Palencia Farm: Finca El Socorro Variety: Laurina Process: washed Notes: toasted almonds, nougat, red apple Brewing: I absolutely love this on espresso! It can hold up well to milk in smaller milk drinks and also makes a very soft and low acidity pourover or aeropress. This coffee has high sweetness but low acidity and low caffeine. It has a complex but subtle profile and works well across differnt brew methods. This combined with the fact that it also has only around 1/3 - 1/2 of the caffeine of other arabica cultivars makes this a very versitile coffee a winner for me! 10oz/285g bags

Colombia, Quindio

Jairo Arcila - banana co-ferment

castillo

Premium
BananaCaramelLime
$27.50 Buy This Bag
Colombia, Quindio · 1500 masl Farmer: Jairo Arcila

Jairo Arcila - banana co-ferment

This coffee is bananas! We've been serving it on espresso with milk at the cafe and it tastes like bananas Foster! Black it tastes like fresh bananas. Yum! Origin: Colombia, Quindio Farm: Santa Monica Altitude: 1500 masl Farmer: Jairo Arcila Variety: castillo Process: banana co-ferment honey process Notes: banana, caramel, lime Brewing: I really enjoy this as the base for a milk drink. You can also blend it with something like our Fazenda Sao Domingos and still get a very banana forward cup. Rest: 3-7 days is good. 6oz/170g bags

Buy Now $27.50
Specialty Coffee

Jairo Arcila - Peach pink bourbon

Premium
Specialty Coffee
$29.00 Buy This Bag
Specialty Coffee · 1450-1500 Cultivar Castillo Location Armenia, Quindio Temperature 18-28 °C Annual rainfall 1800-2000 mm Process honey process with peach This pink bourbon was grown by Jairo Arcila on the Santa Monica farm, Colombia. The cherries were exposed to a dry anaerobic fermentation phase of 72 hours. During this fermentation phase fresh peaches and wine yeast were added. The cherries were then pulped and placed together with ripe peaches to dry on raised beds until the ideal moisture content was reached. The result is a wonderful peach bomb which recreates the experience of biting into a peach with the perfect balance of sweetness and acidity and turns it into liquid form! This co-fermented coffee is distinct and harmonious with the varietal coming through more distinctly as the coffee rests and after brewing as it cools in the cup. Alongside peach, I tasted bergamot, brown sugar, lime, and earl grey. This peach pink bourbon works very well brewed as filter coffee. To boost acidity brew it in a conical brewer for a sweeter more blended cup use an immersion brewer like the aeropress. It's roasted light but because it's a honey process co-fermented coffee, be careful not to overextract it by going at it too hard and/or with too much heat. 6oz/170g bags

Jairo Arcila - Peach pink bourbon

Peachy goodness just in time for spring! Drinking this is really reminiscent of biting into a juicy peach – with the perfect harmony of sweetness and acidity resulting in a mouth-watering cup! This is possibly the best co-ferment we've had yet! Municipality Quindío Altitude 1450-1500 Cultivar Castillo Location Armenia, Quindio Temperature 18-28 °C Annual rainfall 1800-2000 mm Process honey process with peach This pink bourbon was grown by Jairo Arcila on the Santa Monica farm, Colombia. The cherries were exposed to a dry anaerobic fermentation phase of 72 hours. During this fermentation phase fresh peaches and wine yeast were added. The cherries were then pulped and placed together with ripe peaches to dry on raised beds until the ideal moisture content was reached. The result is a wonderful peach bomb which recreates the experience of biting into a peach with the perfect balance of sweetness and acidity and turns it into liquid form! This co-fermented coffee is distinct and harmonious with the varietal coming through more distinctly as the coffee rests and after brewing as it cools in the cup. Alongside peach, I tasted bergamot, brown sugar, lime, and earl grey. This peach pink bourbon works very well brewed as filter coffee. To boost acidity brew it in a conical brewer for a sweeter more blended cup use an immersion brewer like the aeropress. It's roasted light but because it's a honey process co-fermented coffee, be careful not to overextract it by going at it too hard and/or with too much heat. 6oz/170g bags

Specialty Coffee

Jairo Arcila - Strawberry Castillo

we had from Jairo Arcila when we launched. This castillo is similar but more refined and floral in our opinion. I tasted strawberry, cantaloupe melon, lime, brown sugar, pineapple, guava and rose hip. Municipality Quindío Altitude 1450-1500 Cultivar Castillo Location Armenia, Quindio Temperature 18-28 °C Annual rainfall 1800-2000 mm This coffee works very well brewed as filter coffee. For a real strawberry fruit-bomb brew it using immersion brewers like the aeropress or pulsar brewer and for a brighter cup with more pronounced acidity, I'd recommend using a conical brewer like the orea v4 narrow or the good 'ol v60. At the cafe we serve is with milk and it tastes like strawberry cheesecake! 6oz/170g bags

Jairo Arcila - Strawberry Castillo

This coffee is sunshine in a cup! As soon as you grind this coffee the wonderful aroma of plump ripe strawberries fills the room! This castillo was grown by Jairo Arcila on the Santa Monica farm, Colombia. The cherries were exposed to a dry anaerobic fermentation phase of 72 hours. During this fermentation phase strawberries and wine yeast were added. The cherries were then pulped and placed together with ripe strawberries to dry on raised beds until the ideal moisture content was reached. The result is a beautiful example of what co-fermented coffees done well can taste like. The strawberry notes marry well with the quality of the varietal and result in a balanced cup. For those of you who have been customers for a while, you may remember the strawberry pink bourbon variety we had from Jairo Arcila when we launched. This castillo is similar but more refined and floral in our opinion. I tasted strawberry, cantaloupe melon, lime, brown sugar, pineapple, guava and rose hip. Municipality Quindío Altitude 1450-1500 Cultivar Castillo Location Armenia, Quindio Temperature 18-28 °C Annual rainfall 1800-2000 mm This coffee works very well brewed as filter coffee. For a real strawberry fruit-bomb brew it using immersion brewers like the aeropress or pulsar brewer and for a brighter cup with more pronounced acidity, I'd recommend using a conical brewer like the orea v4 narrow or the good 'ol v60. At the cafe we serve is with milk and it tastes like strawberry cheesecake! 6oz/170g bags

Rodríguez de Mendoza, Amazonas, Peru Process Fully washed and dried in the sun Certification Fair Trade (FT FLO/USA) | Organic 10oz/285g bags · 1500-1800 masl Variety Bourbon, Catimor, Caturra, Pache and Typica Region Rodríguez de Mendoza, Amazonas, Peru Process Fully washed and dried in the sun Certification Fair Trade (FT FLO/USA) | Organic 10oz/285g bags

Peru - organic, fairtrade - washed process

This coffee is a real crowd pleaser and full of milk chocolate and nutty goodness! Looking to support women in coffee? For the past five years, Asociación de Productores Cafetaleros de la Cuenca del Rio Marañón (APROCCURMA) has been working directly with 124 women producers to establish coffee production from farms owned and managed by women. This coffee works well as espresso as it has great sweetness and good body. You can drink it as a pure espresso or use it as the base for milk drinks as it cuts through and harmonizes well. It also works great for drip coffee for those who enjoy more chocolate and nut forward offerings. We were getting notes of milk chocolate and nuts, green apple and lime. Grower 124 women members of Asociación de Productores Cafetaleros de la Cuenca del Rio Marañón (APROCCURMA) Altitude 1500-1800 masl Variety Bourbon, Catimor, Caturra, Pache and Typica Region Rodríguez de Mendoza, Amazonas, Peru Process Fully washed and dried in the sun Certification Fair Trade (FT FLO/USA) | Organic 10oz/285g bags

Colombia, Quindio, Armenia

Sebastian Ramirez - blackberry co-ferment

caturra

Premium
Blackberry jamCacao nibsLemon cakeBrown sugar
$28.00 Out of Stock
Colombia, Quindio, Armenia · 1750 masl Farmer: Sebastian Ramírez

Sebastian Ramirez - blackberry co-ferment

Blackberry jam with the tartness of fresh jucy berries! Origin: Colombia, Quindio, Armenia Farm: El Placer Altitude: 1750 masl Farmer: Sebastian Ramírez Variety: caturra Process: blackberry co-ferment with thermal shock Notes: blackberry jam, cacao nibs, lemon cake, brown sugar, blueberry, basil, baked chicoree Brewing: filter or cold brew would be my choice. It's also delicious as the base for cappuccinos and flat whites where it reminds us of blackberry cobbler. We brew it at the cafe on the aeropress with a medium fine grind and a water temperature of 93.5 degrees Celsius. Rest: 1 week minimum, 2 is better. This blackberry co-fermet from the womderful Sebastian Ramirez uses a unique technique that blends the flavor of blackberries with the flavour profile inherent in the coffee beans to result in a blackberry forward but highly balanced cup. This coffee stands out for its tartaric acidity, achieved through controlled fermentation, where the temperature is carefully regulated. After the coffee is depulped, a thermal shock with hot water is applied to halt the fermentation process and lock in the flavors developed up to that point. This innovative method creates a sensory experience reminiscent of fresh blackberry jam, with hints of chocolate sweetness and a touch of blueberries, offering a complex and sophisticated flavor profile. 6oz/170g bags

Colombia, Huila, Pitalito

Yessica & Diego Parra - thermal shock sidra

bourbon sidra

Premium
StrawberryPink grapefruitGummy bearsPassion fruit
$29.50 Out of Stock
Colombia, Huila, Pitalito · 1750 masl

Yessica & Diego Parra - thermal shock sidra

Bold and tropical! This is an amazing tropical and fruit forward coffee that is perfect for those who are looking for adventure! Origin: Colombia, Huila, Pitalito Farm: El Mirador Farmers: Yessica & Diego Parra Variety: bourbon sidra Process: natural thermal shock Elevation: 1750 masl Notes: strawberry, pink grapefruit, gummy bears, passion fruit, papaya, cherry, bright lime Brewing: roasted primarily for filter coffee Rest from roast date: 2-3 weeks ideally. 10 days minimum. This coffee is intense, funky and packed with fruity goodness! It stands out as one of the most unique in Yessica and Diego’s portfolio. The coffee cherries go through an innovative thermal shock fermentation process: a technique that alternates hot and cold water during fermentation to stress the beans in a controlled way, allowing them to absorb more sugars and intensify their sweetness. The results speak for themselves!

In-Person · Ann Arbor Only

The Curious Coffee Club

So many of you have asked for this and we are delighted to offer it! The Curious Coffee Club is open to all those who are able to make it to Ann Arbor.

☕

Two bags a month

Pick up two bags of your choosing from the available stock on the shelves at the cafe each month.

🤝

Monthly meetups

Mid-month meetups to chat with other coffee enthusiasts, taste something new and ask Mike about the coffees you picked up.

🎓

Workshop discounts

10% off workshops and one-to-one sessions. 5% off all further bags bought at the cafe. Plus special events and sneak peeks at upcoming coffees.
